observations.recommendation:
1.16 We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor order the GSO-
Designate to maintain and update records regarding acquisition, description,
custody, estimated life, rate of depreciation, disposal, and other information of PPE
based on the source documents of the transactions and reconcile the same with the
Accounting Records.

1.17 We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant shall update the record of
acquisition/disposal/transfer of PPEs in the PPELCs and ensure that the total
balance of PPELCs tally with the balances of controlling PPE accounts in General
Ledger.

1.18 We further recommended that the Inventory Committee, in coordination with the
GSO and Municipal Accountant to complete the physical inventory within the
prescribed period for the purpose of one-time cleansing of the PPE accounts, and
to undertake the disposition of the non-existing PPEs and unserviceable PPEs in
accordance with COA Circular No. 2020-006.

2.12 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Accountant to:
a) prepare the Subsidiary Ledger for every project under the CIP account
based on the source documents and which shall be reconciled with their
respective control account in General Ledger at the end of every month.
b) prepare a Journal Entry Voucher to reclassify the completed projects from the
CIP to the appropriate PPE account, and henceforth effect the reclassification
of the completed projects upon completion as shown in the Certificate of
Completion; and
c) recognize the corresponding depreciation of the PPE from the date of
completion. Depreciation shall be for one month if the PPE is available for use
on or before the 15th of the month, and on the succeeding month if the PPE is
available for use after the 15th of the month.

3.10 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct:
a) the Municipal Treasurer:
i. to coordinate with the bank to determine the source agency or
name of depositor of the credit memo amounting P100,000.00, as
well as its purpose and liquidation requirements, if any; and
ii. timely prepare the schedule or list of unreleased checks for all
funds at yearend, and submit the same to the Municipal
Accountant within the deadline set by the latter.
b) the Municipal Accountant to prepare the appropriate journal entries
to recognize the bank credit memo and to provide the necessary
disclosures in the Notes to Financial Statements. Henceforth, restore
unreleased checks by preparing a journal entry voucher at the end of
the year based on the list submitted from the MTO. At the start of the
ensuing year, draw another entry to reverse the previous entry made
and recognize the availability of the checks for release.

4.19 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor secure written agreement for every
undertaking involving fiscal administration and direct the:
a) Waterwork supervisor to prepare a complete statement of water meter reading
showing the quantity of water consumed and the corresponding amount due
from each consumer, and submit the same to the MTO and the Municipal
Accountant.
b) MTO to collect the amount due from the water consumers based on the
statement made by the Waterwork Supervisor, and prepare a separate statement
of collections on waterworks to be submitted to the Municipal Accountant;
c) MTO to revise the stipulations in the lease contract which must be consistent
with the provisions of the Revised Revenue Code of the Municipality and
provide the Municipal Accountant with a copy of the Lease Contract of all the
tenants and enforce the collection of 25% surcharges for every late payment of
the rental fees.
d) Municipal Accountant to record the income on an accrual basis based on the
monthly statement of the water consumption for the waterworks and based on
the Lease Contract. And, prepare a subsidiary ledger per tenant. Also, conduct
monthly reconciliation with the MTO records to ensure check and balance and
to allow immediate detection and correction of errors.

5.12 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant
to:
a) reclassify the balances recorded under the Accounts Payable to the appropriate
account as defined in Annex B of COA Circular No. 2015-009 or the Revised
Chart of Accounts for LGUs.
b) Confirm all payables with the payees/ creditors, and settle the same which
should be supported with complete documentation or prepare the necessary
adjusting journal entry voucher, as appropriate, to reflect the actual liabilities of
the municipality.

6.11 We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to strictly enforce the posting of the
warranty security by the contractors after the issuance of the Certificate of
Final Acceptance by the Municipality pursuant to the provisions of the Revised
IRR of R.A. 9184.

6.12 Also, we recommended to the Municipal Accountant to require the warranty
security as one of the documentary requirements for the final payment of
completed projects in compliance Item 9.1.1.4 of COA Circular No. 2012-001.

6.13 The Municipal Engineer commented that, in coordination with the Office of the
Municipal Mayor and Municipal Accountant, the office will strictly implement the
securing of warranty security as supporing document for the final payment to the
contractor and the issuance of Certificate of Final Acceptance.

7.9 We recommended the Municipal Mayor, through the Municipal Development
and Planning Officer and Budget Officer, to ensure that all programs,
projects, and activities to be implemented by the Municipality for the year is
included in the Annual Investment Program (AIP) otherwise a Supplemental
AIP shall be prepared, which to be approved by the local sanggunian through
a resolution.

7.10 We likewise recommended the Municipal Treasurer to refund the undisbursed
financial assistance for Typhoon Odete-affected individuals to the Bureau of
Treasury.

8.16 We recommended the Municipal Mayor to direct the:
a) The Municipal Budget Officer to prepare RCDU per donor and per
purpose, and timely update the registry by posting the transactions based
on the source documents such as the RCD for the receipt and the CDUR for
its utilization.
b) The Municipal Accountant to:
i. Transfer the unexpended balance of the cash donations to the Trust
Fund-DRRM books.
ii. Include in the LDRRM Utilization Report the receipt and utilization of
the cash donations for DRRM.
iii. Require the CDUR, duly signed by the head of the requesting unit and
the budget officer, as an attachment to the Disbursement Voucher on
the utilization of cash donations.
iv. Disclose all relevant information not evident on the face of the FS, such
as but not limited to the donation received, utilization, and balances in
the Notes to Financial Statements.

9.10 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and the Municipal Disaster Risk
Reduction and Management Council to procure the prescribed DRRM tools
and equipment under NDRRMC, DBM, and DILG Joint Memorandum
Circular No. 2013-1 to equip the Municipality in responding to calamities and
strengthen the capacity for disaster risk reduction and management.

10.12 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor adequately stipulate in the Deed
of Sale the terms regarding the payment of taxes and other costs necessary for
the execution and transfer of title.

10.13 We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant comply with the
documentary requirements set under COA Circular No. 2012-001 and conduct a
pre-audit in the processing of claims and review of supporting documents to
determine the completeness of the requirements before effecting payment.

10.14 Finally, we recommended to submit the lacking documents to the Audit Team.

11.16 We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a) Require the HRMO to assess the existing Organizational Structure so
that the ideal number of plantilla personnel will be established and
submit a report with recommendations thereon to the Local Chief
Executive;
b) Create written policies and guidelines in the hiring process and in the
determination of the applicable wage rate for the JO personnel.
c) Comply with the existing daily minimum wage rate for Region VIII, as
prescribed by the RTWPB;
d) Require the Municipal Accountant to process payrolls with complete
documentary requirements and with the complete signatures of the
authorized signatories of the payroll.

12.17 We recommended the Municipal Mayor to require the Public Health Nurse as
Vaccine Coordinator to:
a) Develop and/or enhance measures that will provide for the swift
administration of any vaccines to the eligible population such as but not
limited to putting a premium on communications and advocacy to boost
the confidence and acceptance of the vaccine;
b) Provide for prior registration of persons willing to be vaccinated to
determine the demand for the vaccines, and not to request nor accept
excessive vaccines that cannot be inoculated before the expiry.
c) Develop a distribution plan or strategy appropriate to their situation,
including an inspection process prior to acceptance of vaccines and
timelines to avoid the incidence of expiration of vaccines.

13.8 We likewise recommended the Management to continually make a conscious
effort to accomplish the identified development projects efficiently and
effectively in accordance with rules and regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: (1) order the Inventory
Committee to conduct physical count of all the properties of the Municipality
and submit the RCPPE as of yearend pursuant to 6.1 and 6.2 of COA Circular
No. 2020-006; and (2) temporary relieved the personnel who will conduct the
physical count from their daily task to devote their full time in the conduct of
1.17 We also recommended that the GSO, in coordination with the Accounting
Department;
1. Reconcile its property records with that of the RPCPPE in accordance with
the provisions of Section 6.3 of COA Circular No. 2020-006 and after
completing the reconciliation procedures, the total reconciled balances for
each PPE account shall be established as the correct balance of PPE at year-
end; and
2. Undergo disposal procedures for non-existing/missing PPEs, if there are
any, in accordance with Section 7 of COA Circular No. 2020-006;
1.18 We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
1. Compute the depreciation of all undepreciated assets and draw a journal
entry to set the accounts in order.
2. Together with the GSO, prepare, maintain and update all the necessary
monitoring records such the PCs and PPELCs respectively.
3. Record accounting entries for the recognition of PPEs found at station,
recognition of loss of PPEs, writing-off/dropping from the books of accounts
the remaining balances amounts lumped under the unreconciled SLs of
PPEs, and derecognition of non-existing/missing items.

We recommended that: (1) The Municipal Assessor submits diligently the
printed copy of the Assessment Roll at the end of each year to the Municipal
Treasurer; (2) The Municipal Treasure furnish the Municipal Accountant a
duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and the amount due and
collectible for the year; (3) develop an effective Tax Collection Campaign
program to intensify and maximize collection of Real Property/Special
Education Tax Receivables including from delinquent tax payers; and (4) The
Municipal Accountant record the delinquent and/or collectible Real Property
Tax and Special Education Tax both in General Fund and Special Education
Fund, and set up Receivables at the beginning of the year.
2.11 We also recommended that Municipal Mayor mandate every employee of the
Municipal Government to assist the Municipal Treasurer’s effort in the
campaign to increase collections of real property taxes to increase the income
and improve the financial capacity of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant
1. Restore unreleased checks by preparing a journal entry voucher at the end
of the year based on the Schedule submitted from the Municipal Treasury
Office.
2. At the start of the ensuing year, draw another entry to reverse the previous
entry made and recognize the availability of the checks for release.
3.15 Finally, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the collectors to
remit the collections daily before 5:00 PM or before leaving the office in case of
emergency leave, and direct the Municipal Treasurer to deposit intact all her
collections, as well as collections turned over by the Revenue Collecting Clerks,
daily or not later than the next banking day.

We recommended that Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to record
subsequent procurement of supplies and materials in the proper
Inventory account upon receipt of the delivery and recognize the related
expenses only based on the Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued (SSMI)
from the Supply Officer duly supported with pre-numbered Requisition and
Issue Slip.
4.11 We also recommended that the Supply Officer prepare the consolidated SSMI
every 15th day of the month, duly supported with the RIS and SSMI received
from the requisitioning offices and submit the consolidated SSMI to the
Accounting Office for recording.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor and members of the Municipal
Development Council assess the attainability and necessity for the continuance
of the unimplemented projects and consider re-programming, if warranted.
5.18 We also recommended that the Municipal Planning Development Officer
prepare quarterly reports on the utilization of 20% Development Fund; and
submit to the Local Finance Committee prior to budget preparation the
unimplemented projects and the balances of the appropriations for further
review and possible re-programming and inclusion in the annual budget.
5.19 Also, we recommended that the Municipality Engineer and all focal person
submit immediately to the BAC Secretariat the Program of Work or Project
Proposal of all projects, programs and activities (PPAs) identified in the AIP
especially those funded in prior years so that the same can be scheduled
immediately for bidding and implementation.
5.20 We also recommended the Municipal Budget Officer:
1. Prepare diligently the Status of Appropriation, Allotments, Obligations and
Balances reflecting the obligations incurred to give picture on the efficiency
and effectiveness of the rate of utilization of appropriations; and (2) Reconcile
with the Municipal Accountant on the true and actual balances of the
continuing appropriations.

We recommended that the Mayor, through the Bids and Awards Committee,
secure approval or authority from the Sangguniang Bayan to enter into
contract prior to entering a contract with a winning bidder in cases of lump
sum appropriation containing generic or general terms or authority to charge
under the 20% Development Fund.
6.9 We also recommended the Municipal Development Council to convene and
identity the specific projects to be undertaken and revised the Annual
Investment Program for 20% Development Fund.
6.10 Lastly, we recommended the Municipal Budget Officer to stop aggregating the
budget into single and generic or general term to avoid legal repercussion.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer:
1. Prepare justification that the 422 calendar days extension of the
contractor is proper and in order.
2. Ensure that all conditions before the start of construction have been
addressed and that a certification shall be issued that detailed
preliminary engineering has been conducted prior to the start of
procurement activities to ensure that necessary requirements and
documents have been completed for the project to be completed as
planned;
3. Examine thoroughly contractor requests for time extensions based on
complete and relevant documents that will allow the reasonableness of
the reasons mentioned.
7.17 Henceforth we recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
1. Direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) (a) to properly plan the
procurement of infrastructure projects; (b) to initiate the forfeiture of
Performance Security of erring contractors in favor of the
Municipality in case of non-completion of projects within the contract
period; and (c) to initiate the sanction of blacklisting erring contractors, if
warranted; and
2. Require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to see
to it that liquidated damages will be effected on every day of delay until the
project is completed and accepted by the officials concerned.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant, Budget Officer and
MDRRMO reconcile their records prior to submission of Annual Report and
make necessary adjustments in the beginning balances of CY 2022.
We also recommended the Municipal Accountant prepare Disbursement
Voucher immediately to transfer the current unexpended LDRRMF
amounting P73,644.15to the appropriate account under the Trust Fund.
Henceforth, prepare the accounting entries for receipt and issuance of goods
from LDRRMF transactions in accordance with the prescribed standards.
Finally, we recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer maintained
Separate Registry of Appropriations, Allotments and Obligations (RAAO) for
Quick Response Fund (QRF), MOOE, and Capital Outlay (CO).

We recommended that the Municipal Social and Development Officer together
with the Property Supply Officer:
(a) Conduct physical count of all the remaining donated items;
(b) Prepare inventory list of the remaining donated items for DRRM every
month or every quarter or until all donations are fully distributed; and (c)
Henceforth, prepare all the reports and documents required to account and
monitor the receipt and issuances of all donations in kind and submit a copy
of all the reports to the Audit Team.
We recommended that the Municipal Supply Officer furnish the Municipal
Accountant with the duplicate copies of all Acknowledgment Receipts of in-
kind donations, invoice receipt and inventory list of donated items for DRRM.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant summarize data from
the documents submitted by the Municipal Social and Development Officer and
Supply Officer compare records with the Registry of Donated Relief Goods for
DRRM maintained by the Municipal Social and Development Officer for check
and balance, and disclose the necessary information in the Notes to the Financial
Statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:
(a) Municipal Health Officer provides justification on the necessity and inclusion
of Barangay Tanod, Punong Barangay, and Kagawad as Health Worker and
in the payment of Special Risk Allowance. This is without prejudiced to the
issuance of Notice of Disallowance;
(b) Rural Health Unit personnel and other concerned offices to submit lacking
supporting documents/ records/ orders to the Audit Team for payment of
SRA and AHDP and accordingly observe compliance on the documentary
requirements for these kinds of personnel incentives to avoid suspension in
audit; and
(c) Municipal Accountant ensures all necessary supporting documents are
attached to claims before approving all payments. Henceforth, only
disbursement vouchers/payrolls with complete supporting documents should be
processed for payment.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor through the
1. Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer submit list of insurable
properties with their correct total cost to the Audit Team.; and
2. Municipal General Services Officer to file insurance for the uninsured
properties with the GSIS consistent with the provisions of Sections 2 and 5
of RA No. 656 and Section 3.1 of COA Circular No. 92-390 dated November
17, 1992.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
1. Require the contractor pay to the Municipal Government the amount of
P223,774.38 for the amount of insurance paid to GSIS. This is without
prejudiced to the issuance of Notice of Disallowance; and
2. Strictly require the contractor to obtain the necessary insurances for the
ensuing years throughout the duration of the contract.
We also required the Municipal Accountant to make an adjusting entry
recognizing Receivable and recognizing prior period error.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the members of the BAC to give utmost priority to the bidding process and shall be on a “jury duty” until the Notice of Award is issued by the HoPE in order to complete the entire procurement process at the earliest possible time.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
Require the  Municipal Treasurer to send  written notices to the payees one month before the checks become stale. Stale checks shall be reported and attached to the RCI prepared at the period of cancellation;
Require the  Municipal Accountant to prepare  the necessary adjusting entries for the stale checks over 6 months but not over 2 years in the total amount of P297,749.53 and  the erroneous recording of P48.00 under the PhilHealth Capitazation Fund by drawing the following entries:
For stale checks over 6 months but not over 2 years:
DR: Cash in Bank-LCCA			P 297,749.53
CR: Accounts Payable 				P 297,749.5
For unadjusted book erros
DR: Cash in Bank – LCCA			P 48.00
CR:Prior Years Adjustment* 			P 48.00
(*to represent surplus adjustment)
Require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to conduct regular reconciliation of the cash book and GL balances to ensure completeness and accuracy of the recorded transactions and the balances of  the Cash in Bank account as of a given period.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor :
Require the GSO to submit the distribution list of the unaccounted medicines with a total cost of P103,208.00; and
Require the Accountant to verify actual existence of the reported balance of Drugs and Medicines and the remaining inventory balance with the RHU. Request a certified copy of the actual balance of the inventory account to be used as basis in correcting/adjusting the balance of the Drugs and Medicines account at the end of the year.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
Require the Inventory Team to complete the physical count of all properties as at year-end and prepare and submit the RPCPPE not later than January 31 of the following year to the Audit Team and periodically conduct physical count of PPEs which should be done annually;
Require the GSO-designate to maintain property cards for each class of property, plant and equipment, and reconcile its records with the Municipal Accountant;
Require the Accountant to maintain and up-date Subsidiary Ledgers for each group of PPEs; and
Require the Accountant to verify the variances between the GL balances and the Lapsing Schedule and prepared the necessary adjustment/corrections.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Accountant to update all PPE records and compute the appropriate depreciation expenses on all Properties, Plant and Equipment using the straight-line method.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to require the Accountant to prepare journal entries for the transfer of PPEs under the Trust Fund to the General Fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant to draw the entry to correct the understatement of Loans Payable-Domestic account.  The Municipal Accountant should coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer in securing bank confirmation on loan balances so that periodic reconciliation between books and bank records be made and effect the necessary adjustments, if any, in the book of accounts so that financial statements of the Municipality is fairly presented.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to:
Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to correct the erroneous entries made for a fair presentation of balances in the financial statements;
Require the Municipal Treasurer to return any unutilized balance of the BGCM Funds after the State of Calamity is lifted.
Require the RHU to submit the list of recipients indicating therein, the dates, names and addresses of beneficiaries for the payments of catering services. The list must include details on the actual time of arrival and departure from the facilities of quarantine Locally Stranded Individual (LSIs), Returning Overseas Filipino Workers (ROF), and logbook/DTR showing the time and date of duty of Frontliners.
Direct the MDRRMO to submit the list of recipients of the distributed 2,315 bags of rice complete with details on quantity received and signatures of the recipients.
Require Municipal Accountant to verify completeness of data in the supporting documents attached to disbursements vouchers such as Purchase Orders, Inspection Reports, etc. before these are forwarded to the Municipal Treasurer for payment;
Require the Members of the Sangguniang Bayan to enact an ordinance creating a special account in the General Fund for Development projects funded from the share of the local government unit concerned in the internal revenue allotment and such other special accounts which may be created by law or ordinance. pursuant to Section 313 of R.A. 7160.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to:
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to issue an Official Receipt for all cash donation received.
Direct his authorized representative to issue Acknowledgement Receipt for the receipt of in-kind donations.
Direct the General Service Officer (GSO) to record the delivered items in Supply Ledger Card (SLC), released supplies supported with Requisition and Issue Slip (RIS) and record issuance in the SLC, and consolidate the RIS in the Report of Supplies and Materials Issued (RSMI) and forward to Accounting Unit.
Require the GSO together with the members of Inventory Committee to include In-kind Donations received by the Municipality in the physical count and Inventory Report, and;
Submit the report on the receipt, distributions, and balances for in-kind donations and the report of receipt and utilization of cash donations together with their supporting documents as a special report to the COA Auditor.

We commended the Management for procuring goods and medicines during calamities that were within the suggested prices of DTI and DOH and we also recommend management to continue to adhere with the rules of the Price Act Law.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-001 and exercise due diligence in the processing of claims and complete documentation must be observed before effecting payment.
Direct the Municipal Accountant and the BAC Secretariat to submit the lacking documents to the COA Auditor for evaluation without prejudice on the issuance of notice of suspension.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to reconcile entries in the cashbook and general ledger at least on a quarterly basis and trace of any material variance and significant findings should be resolved promptly.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Accountant, General Services Officer and Municipal Engineer to strictly follow the accounting and reporting procedures mentioned in  COA Circular No. 2015-008 and COA Circular No. 2016-004 for their guidance in making necessary journal entries to record back to Property, Plant and Equipment all Public Infrastructures that was transferred to Registry of Public Infrastructure.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Accountant, Treasurer, Assessor, and GSO to coordinate with each other and look for records relative to the proper recognition and recording of the parcels of land in the books of accounts
We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to implement the Checkless Payment System via Automated Teller Machines (ATMs).

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Accountant to:

Send demand letters to the debtors for the immediate settlement of receivables with the signature of the Mayor; andrequest authority from the COA for the write off of dormant Due from NGAs, Due From LGU, and Other Receivable accounts in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016, if warranted, duly supported by a quarterly aging of dormant receivable and other relevant documents.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Accountant to:
request authority from COA to revert to unappropriated surplus the balances for Due to Philhealth and Due to GOCCs Accounts that were dormant for the past nineteen years amounting to P21,914.52 and P17,699.49 respectively;

secure from the GSIS the list of municipal employees with arrears in loan payments from 2015 and downwards. Reconcile it with the available municipal records, such as payrolls, for the amount withheld and remitted to GSIS as payments of individual loans. Pay the GSIS for any underremittance of amount withheld for salary and policy loans and refund the employees who voluntary pay the GSIS; and

undertake thorough review and analysis of the details of the Due to LGU accounts and send confirmation letter to the Provincial Government of Southern Leyte and to the 29 barangays of the municipality for any unremitted RPT share in 2016 and downwards. Effect the necessary adjustments to revert to the appropriated funds, after securing authorization from COA, the balance of liability accounts which could not be supported with valid claims.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Accountant to provide sufficient appropriate disclosure on the Loans Payable-Domestic account in the Notes to Financial Statements to provide sufficient and adequate information.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Accountant:
to conduct regular and periodic verification, analysis, and validation of the existence of fund transfers; and

to reconcile the unliquidated fund transfers with the source agencies and prepare adjusting entries for the reconciling items noted, and require liquidation of the balances.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Engineer to ensure that all conditions preliminary to the commencement of construction have been addressed and that a certification shall be executed that detailed preliminary engineering has been conducted prior to the start of procurement activities to ensure that necessary requirements and documents have been undertaken for the project to be completed as planned.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Human Resource Officer:
to recommend guidelines to address the issue on the necessity and propriety of hiring JO workers taking into consideration the existing regular employees;

to limit the hiring of personnel under Job Order contracts to those extremely necessary and for services such as janitorial, security, or consultancy to accommodate the increase of daily rate pursuant to the minimum wage required under Wage Oder No. RB VIII-21, and deploy only JO workers to offices that actually are in need of their services.  

to incorporate in the contract the number of man hours, quantity and duration of the work to be performed by the JO worker and require them to submit quantified accomplishment reports to support payment of wages.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Local Finance Committee to adhere to Paragraph (a) and (b) of Section 316 of RA No. 7160 which requires that budget should be lodged in the income reasonably projected and collectible for the ensuing fiscal year and that appropriately support the budget.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to direct the GAD Focal Person to conduct gender analysis in the formulation of programs and projects and activities so that the intended beneficiaries are assured of the services, protection and other assistance that may be provided.  Likewise, programs for the SCs and PWDs should be separated from the GAD Programs and Budget.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Accountant to:
issue demand letter with the signature of the Mayor to the payees/accountable officers concerned with unsettled disallowances;

enforce withholding of payment or any amount due to the persons liable to settle said disallowances; and

ensure that all employees who are retiring or transferring to other agencies shall first settle the disallowances and charges for which they are liable in accordance with Section 7 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.
We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Health Officer to facilitate the licensing of its ambulance with the DOH.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Assessor to registered all lands owned by the Municipality under the Torrens Title System pursuant to COA Circular No. 92-386

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the BAC Chairman, Municipal Engineer, MPDC, Municipal Agricultural Officer and Municipal Social Welfare Development Officer to judiciously plan proposed projects and fast-track the implementation of said programs, projects and activities funded by the BUB, AM, ECCD, and LGSF to provide constituents of the benefits due them.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the HRMO to adhere to the provisions of Section 77 of RA No. 7160 which provides that the Municipality may hire Job-order for local projects only, authorized by the Sangguniang Bayan.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Person to see to it that programs, projects and activities are aligned with GAD-related undertakings, so that the intended beneficaries are assured of the services, protection and other assistance that may be provided.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor compel the Municipal Accountant to review the details of the Due from NGAs, Due from LGUs, Due from Other Funds, and Loans Receivable Accounts and exert all efforts to identify the purpose of the fund transfers as well as the recipients of said funds.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to exert effort in enforcing settlement of audit disallowances. Ensure that all employees who are retiring of transferring to other agencies shall first settle the disallowances and charges for which they are liable in accordance with Section 7 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct  the Municipal Accountant to a) analyze the accounts with balances that remained unreconciled and prepare the necessary adjusting entries to correct the account balance and b) coordinate with the Municipal Treasurer to identify the employees with unremitted contributions to enable the Municipal Government to settle its obligations.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Treasurer to conduct a complete physical count of Property, Plant and Equipment so as to arrive at a reconciled balance with accounting records.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit monthly Bank Reconciliation  Statements  on  time  in  order to check the accuracy the balances of  the  bank  accounts and  to submit the  journal vouchers to ensure proper recording of all valid reconciling items that require adjustment/correction in the GL.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Budget Officer to strictly adhere with the provisions of the DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum No. 2017-1 on the appropriation and utilization of the 20% of the IRA.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive compel the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator to: a) re-evaluate the operation, billing and collection process of the water system project and identify the problems that hampered its operation and collection; b) effect immediately the necessary corrective measures; and c) initiate strategies to collect unpaid water bills from consumers who are remiss in paying their obligations to the LGU.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: a) Direct the Municipal Treasurer to compel the Driver to prepare a properly filled-up Driver’s Trip Ticket days before the scheduled official travel and prepare Monthly Report of Official Travels duly approved by the Municipal Treasurer  and submit to the Auditor within five (5) days after the end of each month;  b) Require the Municipal Treasurer to prepare a monthly Report of Fuel Consumption duly supported with approved and properly accomplished Driver’s Trip Tickets and approved by him (LCE)  and submit to the Auditor for audit five days after end of each month; and c)  Compel the Municipal Accountant to strictly adhere to the practice of checking as to completeness of documentation before claims are to be paid.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Local Budget Officer to strictly adhere to the provisions of Section 15 of Republic Act 9344 known as Juvenile Justice and Welfare Act of 2006 and appropriate the required allocation of 1% of its IRA for the LCPC activities. Furthermore, the LCE should cause the creation of the Local Council for the Protection of Children and direct the said council to prepare its Annual Work and Financial Plan for the attainment of the LCPC projects and activities for the welfare of the children.

Direct the BAC and the Municipal Accountant for stringent checking and cross-checking the documentation of disbursements; and 3.)  Direct the Municipal Treasurer – Designate to stop payment of claims lacking or deficient of the required documentation.

We recommended that the Local Chief  Executive direct the Municipal  Accountant  to regularly  reconcile  the amounts withheld and remitted to GSIS in order to ascertain  the accuracy and reliability  of the balances  on  Due to GSIS in the  financial statements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the GAD Focal Person to prioritize the implementation of the CBMS for the creation of GAD database to serve as input or basis for planning, programming and policy formulation.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive should consider creating and organizing an Internal Audit Services, in compliance with R.A. No 4177 and AO 70 to give assurance for a sound and adequate internal control system in the operations of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require each program  and projects  coordinator  to fast-track the implementation  of programs and projects funded by the  National  Government in accordance  with the timeline provided for in the  MOA  and require  them  to submit  the Liquidation  Reports and other documents to the Source  Agency  immediately.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: a) Direct the Local Risk Reduction Management Council to prepare its financial Plan of Program/Activities and Utilization Report in accordance with the provision of Section 5.0 of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 dated March 25, 2013 and                  b) Compel the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant     not the charge the expenses for meals and snacks or catering services to the LDRRM Funds.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: a) Direct the Human Resource Management Officer to review the duties and functions of the Job Order Workers so as not to duplicate the duties and functions of the regular plantilla employees; and b) Compel the Municipal Budget Officer to adjust the rates of Job Order Workers in compliance with regulations.

We recommended that the LCE require the BAC to strictly adhere to Section 37.4.2 of RA 9184, i.e., to  post in the PhilGEPS Notices of Award of Contracts, approved Contracts of Agreement, and Notice to Proceed for transparency and information in all procurement processes.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE)  require the SWM Focal Person and management board the establishment of the standard-based Material Recovery Facility (MRF) and adequate dissemination in the operation and use of the MRF.  Also require the remaining barangays to establish their Material Recovery Facility in support to the LGUs ecological solid waste management program.
We also recommended that the LCE provide the necessary financial and technical support in enjoining the barangays to establish their own MRF according to standards and strictly implement waste segregation
We recommended that contracts of job order workers should contain statements of duties and responsibilities or equivalent term to serve as standard with which to base the accomplishment.

We recommended that the management should base their converted accounts especially for inventories and PPE on the physical inventory reports submitted by the GSO or the Municipal Treasurer in order to reflect a reliable and accurate amount to be presented in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant must analyze the cause of the abnormal balances and draw a Journal Entry Voucher to adjust the affected accounts in order to reflect the correct balances in the books of the agency.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive strictly impose on the Municipal Accountant-Designate and the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer the preparation of the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF.
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Property Officer in-charge to submit the Report of Property Plant and Equipment as of December 31, 2015.

We recommended management to intensify its effort to collect targeted collection goals using demand letters and the enforcement of penalties against delinquents, in order to augment LGU funds for various development projects.

We recommended for the submission of GAD Plan and other necessary reports to concerned agencies including the presentation for audit by the accounting office of financial reports and documentations on fund utilization. While not yet complied with, GAD implementation cannot be considered valid and the corresponding disbursement vouchers maybe subjected to suspension or disallowance as the case maybe.

We recommend that the agency should adhere strictly to COA Circular No. 2012-003 dated October 29, 2012-Annex E and DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2004-130 dated October 1, 2004 in order to prevent wastage of government funds and observe austerity measures in all government transactions.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct officials concerned to conduct annual physical inventory which shall be reported on the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) to be submitted to COA not later than January 31, of each year.
Likewise, we recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct that Municipal Accountant-Designate to maintain Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards (PPELC) for each category of assets and the General Services Officer-Designate to maintain Property Cards of all PPEs by type to facilitate the reconciliation of accounts.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant-Designate to record individual employee’s ledger for Philhealth contributions / deductions to support the balances in the subsidiary and general ledgers and regularly reconcile the amounts withheld for and remitted to Philhealth in order to ascertain the accuracy and reliability of the balance of Due to Philhealth in the financial statements pursuant to Sec. 111 (2) of PD 1445.

We recommend that the that the Municipal Mayor to direct the Municipal Accountant-Designate to maintain individual employee’s ledger for GSIS contributions/deductions to support the balances in the subsidiary and general ledgers and regularly reconcile the amounts withheld for and remitted to GSIS in order to ascertain the accuracy and reliability of the balance of Due to GSIS in the financial statements pursuant to Sec. 111 (2) of PD 1445.

We recommended that Management should strictly observed pertinent provisions of the NGAS Manual with regard to recognition, monitoring and control of payable accounts.
Further, we recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant-Designate to effect the necessary accounting entries to revert prior years’ accounts payable where no actual claims, whether administrative or judicial, have been filed. No obligation shall be certified to accounts payable unless it is founded on a valid claim that is properly supported by sufficient evidence and unless there is proper authority for its incurrence pursuant to Section 4(s) Volume I of NGAS Manual and Section 157 Volume 1 of GAAM.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor should : (1) create a property inventory team who will undertake the physical inventory taking of all PPEs of the LGU (2) strictly implement the provisions of Sections 122 of PD 1445 and 124 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, Vol. 1 and require them to immediately submit the RPCPPE to the Audit Team (3) designate a General Services Officer and require him/her to prepare and maintain a Property Cards of all PPES and the balance should always reconciled with the ledger cards of the accounting unit of the LGU.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Assessor, Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to secure copies of the documents evidencing the ownership of the land allegedly owned by the LGU so that proper valuation could be determined and recorded.
Constitute a committee to conduct physical inventory of LGU properties, appraise their values and prepare the report pursuant to Sec. 124 of NGAS Vol. 1.

The Municipal Mayor should require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to strictly observe Sec. 100 and 107 of PD 1445 otherwise the penal provisions under Sec. 127 of PD 1445 shall be applied.

The Municipal Accountant should make monitoring of accounts received in the Trust Fund as well as its disbursements in order to avoid committing the same error and its is also suggested that he should review the previous entries that contributed to the negative balances so that a proper adjusting entry could be made.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Accountant
and the Municipal Treasurer to reconcile entries in the cashbook and general
ledger at least on a quarterly basis and to trace of any material variance and
significant findings should be resolved promptly.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal
Accountant, General Services Officer and Municipal Engineer to strictly follow
the accounting and reporting procedures mentioned in COA Circular No.
2015-008 and COA Circular No. 2016-004 for their guidance in making
necessary journal entries to record back to Property, Plant and Equipment all
Public Infrastructures that was transferred to Registry of Public Infrastructure
so that fair presentation of the financial statements could be attained.

Accountant to reevaluate the listings of completed infrastructure projects in
the registries and reclassify those infrastructure projects as repairs and
maintenance those that do not partake the nature of an asset account. Also,
improvement/repairs and maintenance of barangay properties that were
included in the registries should be properly reclassified as donations.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor:
a. to require the Municipal Accountant, Treasurer, Assessor, and GSO to
coordinate with each other and look for records relative to the proper
recognition and recording of the parcels of land in the books of accounts;
and
b. to require the Municipal Accountant to observe the provisions of Section
63 of PD No. 1445 and Paragraphs 26 and 27 of PPSAS 17 on the proper
measurement on the recognition of the parcels of land and make
correcting/adjusting entries.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to:
a. require the Municipal Accountant to determine the cost of the road lot and
recognize it as part of the road network component including the cost of
constructing the drainage and slope protection and other miscellaneous
structure, if there is any;
b. cause the creation of an Inventory Committee and require the Committee
to conduct an inventory of all the local roads under its jurisdiction, prepare
and submit a verified Report on the Physical Count of the Road Network
System (RPCRNS);
c. require the General Services Officer, Municipal Engineer and Municipal
Accountant to reconcile their respective records; and
d. require the Municipal Accountant to maintain and update subsidiary
ledgers. and disclose in the notes to financial statements the local road
network system as prescribed by regulation.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Accountant:
a. to conduct regular and periodic verification, analysis, and validation of the
existence of fund transfers;
b. to reconcile the unliquidated fund transfers with the source agencies and
prepare adjusting entries for the reconciling items noted, and require
liquidation of the balances.
We further recommended to the Municipal Mayor:
a. to require the Municipal Treasurer to disburse funds strictly in accordance
with the work and financial plan timelines, otherwise, a revised and
approved disbursement schedule shall be submitted; and direct the
Municipal Treasurer to refund the Trust Fund for the excess disbursement
made out of the Trust Fund totaling P58,664.78; and
b. to require the Municipal Officials concerned to properly plan the projects to
be implemented and to make sure that funds disbursed is in accordance
with the approved line items budget of the projects funded by the fund
transfers to avoid over spending.

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Management to
cause the implementation of a Payroll System to distribute salaries via
Automated Teller Machines (ATMs).

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal
Accountant to:

accounts such as (a) approved loan application of those with unpaid
loan balances and (b) subsidiary ledger of the individual person who
availed of the loan especially those with unpaid loan balances;
b. send demand letters to the debtors for the immediate settlement of
receivables with the signature of the Mayor; and
c. request authority from the COA for the write off of dormant Due from
NGAs, Due From LGU, and Other Receivable accounts in accordance
with COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016, if
warranted, duly supported by a quarterly aging of dormant receivable

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Accountant
to provide sufficient appropriate disclosure on the Loans Payable-Domestic

adequate information so that the liquidity and solvency of the municipality

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the members of the

We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the Disaster Risk

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
 Direct the Municipal Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical count of PPE and come up with the RPCPPE.
 Direct the General Service Officer-Designate and the Municipal Accountant to determine the validity of the PPE account through reconciliation of the accounting records and the actual inventory. Any adjustment in the RPCPPE should be properly identified.
 Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the PPE Ledger Card (PPELC) for each class of PPE listing therein the data as to its acquisition, description, custody, estimated life, rate of depreciation, disposal, and other information about the property, plant and equipment, based on the source documents of the transactions and drop from the books of accounts the unserviceable properties after the Waste Material Report and the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property are duly approved and evaluated by the COA.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant:
 to comply with the provisions of Sections 74 and 111 of PD No. 1445 in the preparation of monthly bank reconciliation statements for all bank accounts maintained by the Municipality and make written representations with the depository banks to ensure prompt submission of the bank statements;
 to draw the necessary journal entries to correct errors that affects the Cash in Bank accounts; and
 to return the outstanding checks for more than two (2) years back in the books as debit to cash in bank and credit prior year’s adjustment duly supported with necessary required documents.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
 Require the Municipal Assessor to register all real estate owned by the Municipality under the Torrens Title System pursuant to COA Circular No. 92-386. Likewise, instruct him to create an appraisal committee to conduct assessment of the value of donated land, if any, to facilitate recording in the books of accounts of the municipality;
 Direct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to determine the validity of the land account through reconciliation of the accounting records and the actual inventory; and
 Request the Sangguniang Bayan to appropriate funds needed to secure TCTs for the real estate of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Budget Officer to ensure that only projects aligned with the purpose/objectives of the 20% Development Fund be funded from such appropriation. The Municipal Accountant should likewise see to it that expenses charged against the 20% DF are in accordance with the prescribed guidelines.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
 Direct the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council (LDRRMC) to prepare a comprehensive risk reduction and management plan in accordance with the provisions of Section 5.0 of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 dated March 25, 2013;
 Compel the Municipal Budget Officer (MBO) to use the 70% of LDRRMF only for those expenses that specifically support risk reduction and management activities of the municipality; and
 Require the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Officer and MBO to judiciously consider the economical aspect of any item to be included in the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Plan.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the BAC Chairman, Municipal Engineer, MPDC, Municipal Agricultural Officer and Municipal Social Welfare Development Officer to judiciously plan proposed projects and fast-track the implementation of said programs, projects and activities funded by the BUB, AM, ECCD and LGSF to provide constituents of the benefits due them.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the MENRO to develop an awareness program on the management of solid waste and choose the appropriate means of communication.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the SWM Focal Person to maintain disposal site records in the RCA.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the HRMO to adhere to Section 77 of RA No. 7160 which provides that the Municipality may hire Job-order for local projects only, authorized by the Sangguniang Bayan.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Person to see to it that programs, projects and activities are aligned with GAD-related undertakings so that the intended beneficiaries are assured of the services, protection and other assistance that may be provided.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
 Compel the Municipal Accountant to review the details of the Due from NGAs, Due from LGUs, Due from Other Funds, and Loans Receivable Accounts and exert all efforts to identify the purpose of the fund transfers as well as the recipients of said funds.
 Direct the Municipal Accountant to comply with the reportorial requirements on funds transferred to local or national government agencies or/and other debtors in order to avoid accumulation of unliquidated and dormant balances of the accounts.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to itemize its estimated income based on the classification in the financial statement during the budgeting process in order to identify which type of revenue needs collection intensification in order to achieve the target.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile its records for loans payable with the MDFO and make necessary corrections in the statement of indebtedness in order to reflect a correct balance of loans payable.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to exert effort in enforcing settlement of audit disallowances. Ensure that all employees who are retiring or transferring to other agencies shall first settle the disallowances and charges for which they are liable in accordance with Section 7 of COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to remit intact all taxes withheld. observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
1. Direct the Municipal Physical Inventory Committee headed by the
Municipal Treasurer to:
a. conduct a complete physical count of Property Plant and Equipment.
b. submit to the Auditor the Report on the Physical Count of Property,
Plant and Equipment (RCPPE) duly reconciled with the accounting
records.
c. list all Property, Plant and Equipment found on hand, classifying them
into serviceable or unserviceable.
d. maintain updated property cards per category on all property plant and
equipment as recorded in the list of Property, Plant and Equipment of
accounting records.
e. reconcile property records ensuring that the balances shown in the
prepared RCPPE agree with the supply records and the book balances
in the accounting records, and vice versa.f. renew and maintain all Property Acknowledgement Receipt (PAR) for
the signature of the holder of each item listed in the prepared RCPPE.g. prepare the necessary Inventory and Inspection Report of
Unserviceable Property and Waste Material Report for the
unserviceable properties found on hand, taking into consideration EO
No. 888 March 18, 1983, COA Memorandum No. 2005-027.
2. Direct the Municipal Assessor to produce a listing of all the properties
owned by the government and reconcile them with those properties
counted by the committee with an explanation for those items that are
recorded in the books but could not be physically located.
3. Direct the ICO-Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to
determine the validity of Property, Plant and Equipment Account through
reconciliation of the actual inventory and accounting records.
4. Require the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Prepare the Property Plant and Equipment Ledger Card (PPELC) for
each class of property, plant and equipment listing therein the data as to
its acquisition, description, custody, estimated life, rate of depreciation,
disposal, and other information about the property, plant and
equipment, based on the source documents of the transactions.
b. Drop from the books of accounts the unserviceable properties after the
Waste Material Report and the Inventory and Inspection Report of
Unserviceable Property are duly approved and evaluated by the COA.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
1. Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit monthly Bank
Reconciliation Statements on time in order to check the accuracy the
balances of the bank accounts and to submit the journal vouchers to ensure
proper recording of all valid reconciling items that require adjustment /
correction in the GL;
2. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to make a formal request to the
depository bank for the release of bank statements with debit / credit
advices within the prescribed regulations; and
3. Direct the Municipal Accountant to conduct periodic reconciliation with
the that Local School Board officials strictly observe the provisions of
Section 272 ofRA7160 and advise the school administrators in their
respective locality to revise their School Improvement Plan to include the
expenses enumerated under DepEd Order No. 12, s. 2014.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the concerned
personnel to strictly adhere with the provisions of the DILG and DBM Joint
Memorandum No. 2017-1 on the appropriation and utilization of the 20% of the IRA
only for rooted development projects which are permanent in nature.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive compel the Municipal
Planning and Development Coordinator to:
a) re-evaluate the operation, billing and collection process of the water
system project and identify the problems that hampered its operation
and collection;
b) effect immediately the necessary corrective measures; and
c) initiate strategies to collect unpaid water bills from consumers who are
remiss in paying their obligations to the LGU.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
1. Direct the Municipal Treasurer to compel the Driver to prepare a properly
filled-up Driver’s Trip Ticket days before the scheduled official travel and
prepare Monthly Report of Official Travels duly approved by the
Municipal Treasurer and submit to the Auditor within five (5) days after
the end of each month;
2. Require the Municipal Treasurer to prepare a monthly Report of Fuel
Consumption duly supported with approved and properly accomplished
Driver’s Trip Tickets and approved by him (LCE) and submit to the
Auditor for audit five days after end of each month; and
3. Compel the Municipal Accountant to strictly adhere to the practice of
checking as to completeness of documentation before claims are to be
paid. Said measure must be carried out to prevent the issuance of audit
suspension or disallowance during post-audit.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Local Budget
Officer to strictly adhere to the provisions of Section 15 of Republic Act 9344 known
as Juvenile Justice and Welfare Act of 2006 and appropriate the required allocation of
1% of its IRA for the LCPC activities. Furthermore, the LCE should cause the
creation of the Local Council for the Protection of Children and direct the said
council to prepare its Annual Work and Financial Plan for the attainment of the LCPC
projects and activities for the welfare of the children.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
1. Direct the Municipal Accountant for the strict adherence to the above
applicable and pertinent regulation;
2. Direct the BAC and the Municipal Accountant for stringent checking
and cross-checking the documentation of disbursements; and
3. Direct the Municipal Treasurer – Designate to stop payment of claims
lacking or deficient of the required documentation.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant to regularly reconcile the amounts withheld and remitted to GSIS in
order to ascertain the accuracy and reliability of the balances on Due to GSIS in the
financial statements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Treasurer to regularly remit the amount withheld to Pag-IBIG to avoid legal
sanction.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the GAD Focal
Person to prioritize the implementation of the CBMS for the creation of GAD
database to serve as input or basis for planning, programming and policy formulation.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive should consider creating and
organizing an Internal Audit Services, in compliance with R.A. No 4177 and AO 70
to give assurance for a sound and adequate internal control system in the operations
of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive compel the Municipal
Health Officer to follow strictly the rules and regulations on the utilization of
Philhealth Capitation Fund in order to achieve the purpose in the creation of the
fund.

We likewise recommended for the Local Chief Executive to cause the
submission of an explanation why said transactions would not be disallowed in audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require each program and
projects coordinator to fast-track the implementation of programs and projects
funded by the National Government in accordance with the timeline provided for in
the MOA and require them to submit the Liquidation Reports and other documents
to the Source Agency immediately.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a) Direct the Local Risk Reduction Management Council to prepare its
financial Plan of Program/Activities and Utilization Report in accordance
with the provision of Section 5.0 of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG Joint
Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 dated March 25, 2013.
b) Compel the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant
not to charge the expenses for meals and snacks or catering services to the
LDRRM Funds.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
1. Direct the Human Resource Management Officer to review the duties and
functions of the Job Order Workers so as not to duplicate the duties and
functions of the regular plantilla employees; and
2. Compel the Municipal Budget Officer to adjust the rates of Job Order
Workers in compliance with regulations.

We recommended that the LCE require the BAC to strictly adhere to Section
37.4.2 of RA 9184, i.e., to post in the PhilGEPS Notices of Award of Contracts,
approved Contracts of Agreement, and Notice to Proceed for transparency and
information in all procurement processes.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the SWM
Focal Person and management board the establishment of the standard-based Material
Recovery Facility (MRF) and adequate dissemination in the operation and use of the
MRF. Also require the remaining barangays to establish their Material Recovery
Facility in support to the LGUs ecological solid waste management program.

We also recommended that the LCE provide the necessary financial and
technical support in enjoining the barangays to establish their own MRF according to
standards and strictly implement waste segregation.
We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Physical Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical count of Property, Plant and Equipment.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Inventory
Committee to:
a. conduct a complete physical count of Property Plant and Equipment.
b. submit to the Auditor the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and
Equipment (RCPPE) duly reconciled with the accounting records.
c. list all Property, Plant and Equipment found on hand, classifying them into
serviceable or unserviceable.
d. maintain updated property cards per category on all property plant and equipment
as recorded in the list of Property, Plant and Equipment of accounting records.
e. reconcile property records ensuring that the balances shown in the prepared RCPPE
agree with the supply records and the book balances in the accounting records, and
vice versa.
f. renew and maintain all Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE) for the
signature of the holder of each item listed in the prepared RCPPE.
g. prepare the necessary Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property
and Waste Material Report for the unserviceable properties found on hand, taking
into consideration EO No. 888 March 18, 1983, COA Memorandum No. 2005-027.
The Municipal Assessor shall likewise produce a listing of all the properties
owned by the government and reconcile them with those properties counted by the
committee with an explanation for those items that are recorded in the books but could
not be physically located.
The ICO-Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant-Designate should
reconcile accounting records and the actual inventory to determine the validity of
Property, Plant and Equipment Account.
The Municipal Accountant-Designate should:
a. Prepare the Property Plant and Equipment Ledger Card (PPELC) for each class of
property, plant and equipment listing therein the data as to its acquisition, description,
custody, estimated life, rate of depreciation, disposal, and other information about the
property, plant and equipment, based on the source documents of the transactions.
b. Drop from the books of accounts the unserviceable properties after the Waste
Material Report and the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property
are duly approved and evaluated by the COA.

We recommended that management strictly require all suppliers post the
prescribed warranty security in compliance to Sec. 62 of the RIRR and RA 9184.
During the exit conference on March 3, 2017, the BAC Chairman said that upon
receipt of AOM No. 2017-007 they already require the suppliers to post warranty
security.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal
Accountant-Designate to strictly observe the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-001
and that all fund transfers received must have a Memorandum Agreement with the
contracting party to include a provision specifying the duties and responsibilities of the
funding agency, the municipality and the third-party beneficiaries. The MOA must also
include a work and financial plan that would identify and address all details of the
project.

We recommended for the submission of justification by the Municipal Budget
Officer and Municipal Accountant-Designate for non-compliance with DILG and DBM
Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1 s. 2011 dated April 13, 2011, requiring the LGU to
utilize the 20% component of the Internal Revenue Allotment (IRA) for development
projects that will contribute to the attainment of a desirable socio-economic development
and environmental management outcomes.
Moreover, programs/projects included in the agency’s Annual Investment Plan for
the 20% Development Fund should be in consonance with the development projects
listed under the DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 1 s. 2011 dated April
13, 2011.
We also recommended that the preparation of program/project guidelines indicating
its rationale, objectives, significance and strategies of implementation to assist the LGU
officials in determining whether such programs/projects were economically and
efficiently expended must be geared towards the achievement of its objectives.

We recommended that Local School Board officials strictly observe the
provisions of Section 272 of RA 7160 and advise the school administrators in their
respective locality to revise their School Improvement Plan to include the expenses
enumerated under DepEd Order No. 12, s. 2014.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive strengthen the MDRRMC by
causing the completion of the required membership of the Council to fully comply with the
provisions of Section 11 of RA 10121 and to get the representation of the private sector
and civil society organizations.
During the exit conference on March 3, 2017, management promised to comply
with the provisions of Section 11 of RA 10121.

We recommended for the strict adherence to the mandates on continuous disaster
monitoring as part of its management of LDRRMF and in the process, use available
resources towards the implementation of projects, programs and activities that are
necessary for the public during emergencies.

We recommended that the BAC, prepare the consolidated Annual Procurement Plan,
duly supported by the Project Procurement Management Plan (PPMP) of each
department/office and the Procurement Monitoring Report (PMR) as required under the
Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Solid Waste
Management Focal Person to: (a) provide the necessary support in enjoining the barangays to establish
their own MRFs and to implement waste segregation at source; (b) strictly intensify the
information campaign on the no-littering and no open burning policies among the residents; and (c)
consider embarking on effective projects and programs to support the LGUs ecological solid waste
management program; (d) strictly comply with all other prescribed procedures and guidelines under
RA No. 9003.

We recommended that management should exert extra effort to prove the existence of the accounts and if effort proved futile, a resolution from the Sangguniang Bayan should be passed to apply for the write off of the accounts which should only be done upon prior authority from COA.
observations.managementAction: LGU has sent demand letters but no reply from the recipient.
